instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Term Plan With Return of Premium Comparison
A term insurance plan is one of the most popular types of life insurance. It offers the financial security of a lump sum pay-out to beneficiaries in the event of death of the life assured. As the duration of the coverage is pre-decided in the policy document, it is referred to as term insurance or term life insurance. When an individual buys term insurance, he/she ensures that their family’s life goals do not get derailed if they are not around.
What are Term Plans with Return of Premium?
A return of premium term insurance plan is a term plan that comes with an additional benefit– return of premium benefit. This means in case the life assured survives the policy term, the life insurance company returns all premiums paid by the life assured, at the end of the policy term. These plans are called Term Insurance with Return of Premium (TROP) option. They come with a relatively higher premium than a pure protection term plan.
Example: If you pay ₹20,000 annually for a TROP plan for 20 years, you’ll receive ₹4,00,000 (₹20,000 * 20) on surviving the policy period. This payout does not cover interest, GST, or rider costs.
What is Regular Term Insurance Plan?
A regular term insurance plan is a type of life insurance bought for a specific period of time. If the life assured passes away during this period, the beneficiary receives the death benefit. There is no maturity payout if the life assured outlives the policy term. This is a suitable plan if you’re the sole breadwinner of the family, as the premiums are comparatively affordable considering the coverage provided.
Differences Between Return of Premium and Regular Term Plans
Let’s take a look at the key differences between term plans with Return of Premium vs Regular Term Insurance:
Benefits of Term Insurance with Return of Premium
Listed below are the benefits of the TROP policy:
ROP On Maturity
If the life assured survives the complete policy term, 100% of the premiums paid are returned as a maturity benefit. This refund can be used to support other life goals or financial commitments.
Death Benefit
Just like regular term insurance, the Return of Premium plan provides the complete sum assured to the beneficiary if the life assured passes away during the policy term and the policy terminates. This ensures that your family is economically supported in tough times.
Tax Advantages
You can claim tax benefits of up to ₹1.5 lakh per year under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act, for the premiums paid. Also, the maturity payout is exempt from tax under Section 10(10D), provided certain conditions are met.
Peace Of Mind with Return Assurance
A TROP provides a death benefit like a regular term plan, along with an additional maturity payout. So, if you outlive the policy period, your premiums are not wasted, but refunded totally to be used in the later years of life.
Financial Planning
Term insurance with ROP blends insurance coverage with a survival benefit, thus motivating you to keep the plan active. It supports financial planning by offering protection, tax savings, and in keeping a safe fund that can be used for other future goals.
Benefits of Regular Term Insurance
A regular term insurance provides the following advantages:
Lower Premium Costs
A regular term insurance plan provides substantial financial protection to your family against a very affordable premium.
Pure Protection Plan
This insurance coverage plan is extremely significant for the breadwinners of the family, as it ensures that their loved ones are not economically handicapped in case of your untimely death.
Add-On Riders
A regular term plan can be enhanced later with add-on riders like disability cover, critical illness, or spouse benefit, thus ensuring financial protection adapts to the evolving life situations.
Better Flexibility
As term plans come with minimum premiums, you can use the additional fund in other investment options that may give higher returns.
Simplicity
The return of premium plan is simple. You pay the premiums and enjoy life insurance coverage during the policy tenure. In the case of death, the death benefit is paid. Alternatively, if the plan matures, the premiums are refunded.
FAQs
Is Term Insurance with Return of Premium (ROP) a Smart Investment?
Term insurance with ROP provides 100% of the premiums paid if the life assured outlives the policy. However, it does not build much of a corpus, as no interest is accrued. It’s wise to consult a financial planner before investing.
What are the Limitations of ROP Term Plans?
Some limitations of the ROP term plan are higher premiums and no returns on the survival benefit. Only the base premium is returned during maturity without covering tax paid on premiums, interest earned, or rider costs (if any).
What Does a Maturity Claim Mean in an ROP Term Plan?
Maturity claim in an ROP term plan is provided if the life assured outlives the policy. The amount equals the total base premiums paid.
What is a Return of Premium (ROP) feature in term insurance?
ROP in term insurance provides the additional benefit of receiving 100% of the premiums paid, if the life assured survives the policy term.
Does ROP term insurance still provide death benefit coverage?
Yes, a ROP term insurance provides a death benefit to the beneficiary, just like a regular term plan, if the life assured passes away during the policy period.
Is ROP term insurance more expensive than regular term plans?
Yes, the premiums paid for return of premium are higher compared to pure term plans.
Will ROP term insurance refund the full premium amount paid?
Yes, if the life assured lives till the end of the policy period, the base premium is completely refunded.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959,  call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212 , mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. The Logo of Bajaj Life Insurance Limited is provided on the basis of license given by Bajaj Finserv Ltd. to use its “Bajaj” Logo.
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 10(10D) and Section 80C (under old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy

output.final_text:
Term Plan With Return of Premium Comparison
A term insurance plan is one of the most popular types of life insurance. It offers the financial security of a lump sum pay-out to beneficiaries in the event of death of the life assured. As the duration of the coverage is pre-decided in the policy document, it is referred to as term insurance or term life insurance. When an individual buys term insurance, he/she ensures that their family’s life goals do not get derailed if they are not around.
What are Term Plans with Return of Premium?
A return of premium term insurance plan is a term plan that comes with an additional benefit– return of premium benefit. This means in case the life assured survives the policy term, the life insurance company returns all premiums paid by the life assured, at the end of the policy term. These plans are called Term Insurance with Return of Premium (TROP) option. They come with a relatively higher premium than a pure protection term plan.
Example: If you pay ₹20,000 annually for a TROP plan for 20 years, you’ll receive ₹4,00,000 (₹20,000 * 20) on surviving the policy period. This payout does not cover interest, GST, or rider costs.
What is Regular Term Insurance Plan?
A regular term insurance plan is a type of life insurance bought for a specific period of time. If the life assured passes away during this period, the nominee receives the death benefit. There is no maturity payout if the life assured outlives the policy term. Term life plans can be suitable if you are the sole earning member of your family because it offers comprehensive cover at relatively low premiums. This makes it easier to secure your family’s financial future without placing a heavy burden on your monthly budget.
Differences Between Return of Premium and Regular Term Plans
Let’s take a look at the key differences between term plans with Return of Premium vs Regular Term Insurance:
Features Term Insurance with Return of Premium
Regular term Insurance
Maturity Benefits
All the premiums paid during the policy are refunded at the time of maturity
No maturity benefit is paid if the life assured survives the policy term. Tax Benefits Apart from tax benefits covered under Section 80C on premiums, the maturity benefit is also exempt from tax under Section 10(10D) , subject to stipulations of the Income Tax Act , 1961.
It offers offer tax benefits under section 80C, of the Income Tax Act, 1961 on the premiums paid. Further, the sum assured the beneficiary gets is tax exempt under Section 10(10D).
Surrender Value A certain proportion of the
premiums paid is given as surrender value, subject to the terms and conditions of the policy.
No payout is provided if the policy is surrendered before the end of the term.
Paid-up Option in case of Non- payment of Premiums
TROP, typicllay includes the paid up option where if the premium is not paid, the policy continues but with reduced sum assured benefit (this is subject to certain minimum number of premiums being paid and depends on policy t&c).
If premiums are missed, the policy lapses and no benefits are provided.
Policy Cover It provides life insurance with return of premium.
It is a life insurance plan with no return of premium feature . Premium The premiums for these plans are higher than pure term plans given the maturity benefit offered.
The premiums are lower than ROP plans because there is no maturity benefit. Suitable For This policy is suitable for people looking for financial security along with maturity payout at the end.
Low premiums make this plan more affordable for people who need financial coverage at an affordable rate.
Benefits of Term Insurance with Return of Premium
Listed below are the benefits of the TROP policy:
ROP On Maturity
If the life assured survives the complete policy term, the premiums paid are returned as a maturity benefit. This benefit can be used to support other life goals or financial commitments.
Death Benefit
Just like regular term insurance, the Return of Premium plan provides sum assured to the nominee if the life assured passes away during the policy term and the policy terminates. This ensures that your family is economically supported in tough times.
Tax Advantages
You can claim tax benefits of up to ₹1.5 lakh per year under Section 80C ( under the old tax regime) of the Income Tax Act, for the premiums paid. Also, the death benefit is exempt from tax under Section 10(10D) , subject to stipulations of the Income Tax Act , 1961..
Peace Of Mind with Return Assurance
A TROP provides a death benefit incase of death of the life assured like a regular term plan, and a maturity benefit if the life assured survives the policy term . So, if you outlive the policy period, your premiums are refunded to be used in the later years of life.
Financial Planning
A Term Insurance with Return of Premium (ROP) plan combines life cover with a return of premiums feature. Since you receive your premiums back at the end of the term, it encourages you to stay invested throughout the policy duration. It supports financial planning by offering financial protection, tax savings, and in keeping a safe corpus that can be used for other future goals.
Benefits of Regular Term Insurance
A regular term insurance provides the following advantages:
Lower Premium Costs
A regular term insurance plan provides substantial financial protection to your family against a very affordable premium.
Pure Protection Plan
This insurance coverage plan is extremely significant for the breadwinners of the family, as it ensures that their loved ones are not economically handicapped in case of your untimely death.
Policy Coverage
You pay the premiums and enjoy life insurance coverage during the policy tenure. In the case of death, the death benefit is paid.
FAQs
Is Term Insurance with Return of Premium (ROP) a Smart Investment?
Whether TROP is a smart investment depends on an individual’s life goals, financial priorities and financial protection needs. Term insurance with ROP provides a return of the premiums paid if the life assured outlives the policy.
What Does a Maturity Claim Mean in an ROP Term Plan?
Maturity claim in an ROP term plan is provided if the life assured outlives the policy. The amount equals the total premiums paid.
What is a Return of Premium (ROP) feature in term insurance?
ROP in term insurance offers return of the premiums paid, if the life assured survives the policy term.
Does ROP term insurance provide death benefit coverage?
Yes, a ROP term insurance provides a death benefit to the nominee , just like a regular term plan, if the life assured passes away during the policy period.
Is ROP term insurance more expensive than regular term plans?
Yes, the premiums paid for term insurance with ROP are higher compared to pure term plans.
Will ROP term insurance refund the full premium amount paid?
Yes, if the life assured lives till the end of the policy period, the base premium is completely refunded.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions.
